title: OmniPrep™ For High Quality Genomic DNA Extraction From Ethanol- or Formalin-Fixed Tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dqttk","text":"The OmniPrep™ kit isolates high quality genomic DNA from many different species and tissue types including animal, plant, bacteria, yeast, fungi, whole blood, and cells in culture. DNA can be isolated from samples high in polysaccharides or other contaminants that are difficult to remove from the DNA preparations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"adv20","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3dnqc","text":"This protocol is for use with ethanol- or formalin- fixed tissue. Please refer to the appropriate protocol depending on your application.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":30,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":86,"length":20}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"24EC248000E7435EAA89D10CD3DEA046","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/OmniPrep-Protocol-Collection-for-High-Quality-Geno-e7rbhm6"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"djshu","text":"INTRODUCTION ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1dpm4","text":"The OmniPrep™ kit isolates high quality genomic DNA from many different species and tissue types including animal, plant, bacteria, yeast, fungi, whole blood, and cells in culture. DNA can be isolated from samples high in polysaccharides or other contaminants that are difficult to remove from the DNA preparations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1072u","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2rio7","text":"Several unique features separate the OmniPrep™ kit from other methods:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6r3n3","text":"• A unique and proprietary formulation of detergents and salts. DNA can be extracted and purified from almost any tissue without the use of toxic agents such as phenol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2jlp1","text":"• A quick protocol, which isolates extremely clean genomic DNA. On an average, the DNA is 100kb in size and generally hydrates in minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tqn","text":"• Provides an option to modify protocol for difficult to handle samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tdkn","text":"• Suitable for 200 Preps (10mg/prep). The kit is adaptable for larger tissue volumes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4qtne","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7dver","text":"ITEM(S) SUPPLIED","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bssga","text":"   Description Cat. # 786-136 Cat. # 786-136S    Genomic Lysis Buffer 100ml 2 x 2ml   Nuclei Isolation Buffer 2 x 30ml -   DNA Stripping Solution 10ml 0.5ml   Precipitation Solution 30ml 2ml   Mussel Glycogen (10mg/ml) 1ml 25µl   TE Buffer 20ml 0.5ml   Longlife™ RNase (5mg/ml; \u003e60U/mg) 0.5ml 50µl   Longlife™ Proteinase K (5mg/ml) 2 x 0.5ml  50µl   ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":15,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":30,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":350}],"data":[]},{"key":"48orq","text":"NOTE: Cat. # 786-136S is a trial/sample size and does not contain enough reagents for all the protocols. Upon arrival, store the kit components as recommended on the reagent label.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d1umq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6f1td","text":"ADDITIONAL REAGENTS REQUIRED","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ni9o","text":"• For all samples: Isopropanol, 70% Ethanol, and Chloroform","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cda7a","text":"• For Gram positive Bacteria: Lysozyme, 0.5M EDTA. Longlife™ Lysozyme, (Cat# 786-037) available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4l3ch","text":"• For Yeast: Zymolyase® , β-mercaptoethanol, phosphate buffered saline (PBS). Longlife™ Zymolyase® (Cat# 786-036) available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cv362","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"egh0q","text":"PREPARATION BEFORE USE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6csl4","text":"Proteinase K Solution: To avoid repeated freezing-thaw, dispense the Proteinase K solution into aliquots of 30µl/tube and freeze at -20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ckbd4","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4f32v","text":"Genomic Lysis Buffer \u0026 DNA Stripping Solution: If a precipitate forms due to cold storage allow to warm to room temperature until precipitate dissolves.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bba7m","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4hlkg","text":"PROTOCOLS ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bcqhs","text":"The protocols contained in this manual are listed below and are based on the Extraction from Solid Tissue protocol. Thoroughly review the appropriate protocols before commencing isolation of genomic DNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ailg","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3sfoa","text":"TROUBLESHOOTING","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eb16g","text":"• POOR DNA RECOVERY:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bei5b","text":"o Increase volumes of Genomic Lysis Buffer, DNA Stripping Solution and Precipitation Solution proportionally.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"863u1","text":"o Introduce optimal Proteinase K step after step 4.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tbtf","text":"o Improve grinding technique. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8u97j","text":"• For efficient grinding of small samples we offer Molecular Grinding Resin™ (Cat. # 786-138), high tensile micro-particles that do not bind nucleic acids and are recommended for grinding and isolation of genomic DNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8f856","text":"• DNA/RNA free matching pestles and microfuge tubes (1.5ml) for grinding small samples are also available (Cat. # 786-138P).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7rud1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dv23i","text":"• FOR \u003e100kb GENOMIC DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ci5s","text":"o Recommend using MegaLong™ DNA isolation kit (Cat. # 786-146, 786-147). Isolates nuclei under mild condition, which are then transferred to Tube-ODIALYZER™ , a new device for DNA isolation. Nuclei are digested with protease followed by dialysis to remove protein and other contaminants. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"abh7t","text":"Proteinase K Solution: To avoid repeated freezing-thaw, dispense the Proteinase K solution into aliquots of 30µl/tube and freeze at -20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"54v9p","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8g3ov","text":"Genomic Lysis Buffer \u0026 DNA Stripping Solution: If a precipitate forms due to cold storage allow to warm to room temperature until precipitate dissolves.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Blot excess fixative from tissue with clean absorbent paper.
Add 5-10mg ground frozen tissue or fresh tissue to a microcentrifuge tube containing 500µl Genomic Lysis Buffer and incubate for 15 minutes at 55-65°C to soften tissue. 00:15:00
Homogenize the sample with a microfuge pestle until a homogenous suspension is acquired, approximately 30-60 strokes.
Incubate the sample at 55-60°C for 15-60 minutes. Do not heat higher than 60°C 00:15:00

OPTIONAL: For maximum DNA recovery, add 1µl Proteinase K solution for every 100µl Lysis Buffer and incubate at 60°C for 1-2 hours. Invert the tube periodically each hour. This step will digest hard to handle tissues and significantly improve the yield. 00:15:00
Allow the sample to cool to room temperature.
Add 200µl chloroform and mix by inverting the tube several times.
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 14,000xg and carefully remove the upper phase to a clean microcentrifuge tube. 00:10:00
Add 50µl DNA Stripping Solution to the sample and invert several times to mix.
Incubate the sample for 5-10 minutes at 60°C. 00:05:00

Add 100µl Precipitation Solution and mix by inverting the tube several times. A white precipitate should be produced, if not add 50µl aliquots of Precipitation Solution until a white precipitate forms.
Centrifuge the sample at 14,000xg for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Transfer the supernatant to a clean tube and precipitate the genomic DNA with 500µl isopropanol.
Invert the tubes 10 times to precipitate the DNA.
OPTIONAL: For increased DNA recovery, add 2µl Mussel Glycogen as a DNA carrier.
Centrifuge at 14,000xg for 5 minutes to pellet genomic DNA. 00:05:00
Remove the supernatant.
Add 700µl 70% ethanol to the tube and invert several times to wash the DNA pellet.
Centrifuge for 1 minute at 14,000xg. 00:01:00 In some samples, the pellet may be hard to see at this point and will be loosely attached to the tube.
Decant or pipette off the ethanol wash.
Invert the tube on a clean absorbent surface for several minutes to allow any excess ethanol to drain away. Do not let the pellet dry completely or it will be difficult to rehydrate.
Add 50µl TE Buffer to the pellet.
Incubate at room temperature for at least 15 minutes to rehydrate. 00:15:00 Incubating the tube at 55-60°C will speed up rehydration. Incubate for 5-60 minutes.
OPTIONAL : 1µl LongLife™ RNase for every 100µl TE Buffer can be added at this stage.
Store DNA at 4°C, for long term storage store at -20°C or -80°C. Typical Yield : 0.5-10µg DNA/10mg tissue
creator: Colin Heath
